title: transport.rs: PoW mining correctly offloaded to spawn_blocking
description: ws_upgrade_and_auth (transport.rs line 453) uses tokio::task::spawn_blocking for mine_token when PoW is required. This is correct: mine_token is CPU-bound and would block the async executor if called directly in an async context. A contributor might suggest removing spawn_blocking to 'simplify' the code or noting that mine_token is 'fast enough'. It is not — difficulty is configurable and at high difficulty values mining can take seconds. The spawn_blocking is load-bearing. Keep it.
